title: Sour Velvety Egyptian Banana Flatbread
description: A tangy, silky flatbread that blends the natural sweetness and mild sourness of ripe banana with a zesty lemon kick, perfect for a monsoonready Egyptian breakfast.

ingredients.name: Ground flaxseed
ingredients.notes: Mix with 6 tbsp water to form a flax‑egg

instructions: In a bowl, whisk together coconut flour, baking soda, salt, and ground flaxseed.
In a separate bowl, combine mashed banana, maple syrup, lemon juice, almond milk, and the flax‑egg mixture until smooth.
Pour the wet mixture into the dry ingredients and stir until a sticky dough forms.
Transfer the dough onto a lightly oiled surface and knead gently for 2–3 minutes until smooth.
Divide the dough into 4 equal balls and cover with a damp cloth; let rest for 10 minutes.
Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium heat and brush with a thin layer of oil.
Flatten each ball into a 5‑inch circle using a rolling pin or by pressing with your palm, then cook 1–2 minutes per side until golden brown and puffed.
Remove from skillet and sprinkle chopped mint over the warm flatbread.
Serve immediately, perhaps with a side of tomato salsa or a drizzle of low‑FODMAP tahini.
Store leftovers in an airtight container for up to 2 days and reheat in the skillet for 30 seconds before serving.
